ontologyAdditions.name: Vertex of a geometric figure
ontologyAdditions.definition: A corner point at which two or more sides, edges or rays of a geometric figure meet.
exclusions: turning point of a function graph

ontologyAdditions.name: Symmetry of cubic graphs
ontologyAdditions.definition: Recognising and using the rotational or point symmetry of a cubic function graph about its point of inflection.

ontologyAdditions.name: Graphs of y = f(|x|)
ontologyAdditions.definition: Constructing and interpreting y=f(|x|) by reflecting the non-negative-x part of y=f(x) in the y-axis.
inclusions: even symmetry created by replacing x with |x|

ontologyAdditions.name: Variance of a data set
ontologyAdditions.definition: The mean squared deviation of data values from their mean, used as a measure of dispersion before taking its square root for standard deviation.
exclusions: variance of an abstract random variable

ontologyAdditions.name: Second-order linear recurrence relations
ontologyAdditions.definition: Solving second-order linear recurrence relations with constant coefficients by an auxiliary equation, complementary function and particular solution.
inclusions: distinct, repeated and complex auxiliary roots

ontologyAdditions.name: Proof of the arithmetic-series sum
ontologyAdditions.definition: Deriving the finite arithmetic-series sum formula rather than only substituting values into it.

ontologyAdditions.name: Proof of the finite geometric-series sum
ontologyAdditions.definition: Deriving the finite geometric-series sum formula by multiplying by the common ratio and subtracting.
inclusions: derivation of the finite sum formula

ontologyAdditions.name: Discrete cumulative distribution function
ontologyAdditions.definition: For a discrete random variable, F(x)=P(X≤x), found by summing its probability mass function over values not exceeding x.
inclusions: cumulative probabilities from a probability table
exclusions: continuous CDF obtained from a density

ontologyAdditions.name: Cluster sampling
ontologyAdditions.definition: Dividing a population into natural groups, selecting one or more whole groups, and sampling members from the selected clusters.

ontologyAdditions.name: Dot plots
ontologyAdditions.definition: A statistical display placing one dot for each observation above its value on a number line so frequencies appear as vertical stacks.

ontologyAdditions.name: Scalar and vector distinction
ontologyAdditions.definition: Distinguishing a scalar, which has magnitude only, from a vector, which has both magnitude and direction.
inclusions: classifying quantities as scalar or vector

ontologyAdditions.name: Equilibrium of connected particles
ontologyAdditions.definition: Applying equilibrium conditions to particles connected by strings or smooth pulleys when the acceleration of each particle is zero.
exclusions: connected particles with non-zero common acceleration

ontologyAdditions.name: Composite numbers
ontologyAdditions.definition: Natural numbers greater than 1 that have more than two positive factors and therefore are not prime.

ontologyAdditions.name: Acceleration-time graphs
ontologyAdditions.definition: Drawing and interpreting acceleration against time for straight-line motion, including the physical meaning of its values and area where appropriate.

ontologyAdditions.name: Projectile range on an inclined plane
ontologyAdditions.definition: Determining the range of a projectile measured to its intersection with an inclined plane and identifying conditions for maximum range.
inclusions: maximum range on an inclined plane

ontologyAdditions.name: Bounding parabola of projectile trajectories
ontologyAdditions.definition: The envelope parabola that bounds all projectile trajectories launched from a fixed point with a fixed initial speed.
inclusions: derivation or use of the envelope

ontologyAdditions.name: Inverse-square law of gravitation
ontologyAdditions.definition: Gravitational force or field strength varies inversely with the square of distance from the attracting mass.

ontologyAdditions.name: Angle of friction
ontologyAdditions.definition: The angle between the resultant contact force and the normal reaction at limiting equilibrium, satisfying tan φ=μ.
inclusions: relation between angle of friction and coefficient of friction
exclusions: angle of repose unless derived under its conditions

ontologyAdditions.name: Vector moment of a force
ontologyAdditions.definition: Representing the moment of a force about a point or axis by the vector cross product r×F and interpreting its direction.
exclusions: scalar force times perpendicular distance only

ontologyAdditions.name: Diagonal of a polygon or solid
ontologyAdditions.definition: A line segment joining two non-adjacent vertices of a polygon, or corresponding non-adjacent vertices of a solid.

ontologyAdditions.name: Angle properties of right-angled triangles
ontologyAdditions.definition: A right-angled triangle has one 90-degree angle, so its other two interior angles are complementary.
inclusions: two acute angles sum to 90 degrees

ontologyAdditions.name: Angle properties of equilateral triangles
ontologyAdditions.definition: Every interior angle of a plane equilateral triangle is 60 degrees.

ontologyAdditions.name: Exponential and logarithmic inequalities
ontologyAdditions.definition: Using logarithms and monotonicity to solve inequalities in which the unknown occurs in an exponent or logarithm.

ontologyAdditions.name: Solving quadratic equations by an appropriate method
ontologyAdditions.definition: Solving a quadratic equation when no single method is prescribed, selecting factorisation, completing the square, the quadratic formula or another valid method.
exclusions: forming a quadratic equation without solving it

ontologyAdditions.name: Partial fractions with an unfactorised cubic denominator
ontologyAdditions.definition: Decomposing an algebraic fraction when a cubic denominator factor is left unfactorised over the coefficient domain used by the qualification.
inclusions: linear numerator over an unfactorised cubic factor where required

ontologyAdditions.name: Derivation of constant-acceleration formulae
ontologyAdditions.definition: Deriving the standard constant-acceleration equations from velocity definitions, averages or integration, rather than only applying them.

ontologyAdditions.name: Odd and even integers
ontologyAdditions.definition: Classifying integers by parity: even integers are divisible by 2 and odd integers leave remainder 1 in absolute parity terms.

ontologyAdditions.name: Matrix powers by diagonalisation
ontologyAdditions.definition: Computing integer powers A^n from a diagonalisation A=QDQ⁻¹ by using A^n=QD^nQ⁻¹.

ontologyAdditions.name: Hyperbolic equations
ontologyAdditions.definition: Solving equations involving hyperbolic functions by exponential definitions, identities or inverse hyperbolic functions.
inclusions: a cosh x + b sinh x = c

ontologyAdditions.name: Linearising nonlinear data relationships
ontologyAdditions.definition: Applying a change of variable to turn a power or exponential relationship into a straight-line form so model parameters can be estimated.
exclusions: linear data already in straight-line form

ontologyAdditions.name: Simultaneous linear congruences
ontologyAdditions.definition: Solving two or more linear congruences for a common integer solution, with the Chinese remainder theorem available when its conditions apply.
inclusions: ax congruent to b modulo n

noChangeDispositions.reason: The official mathematical-practice requirements are already linked to logical reasoning, symbolic notation and communication nodes; they do not require another mathematical-knowledge leaf.

The atomic requirement is already linked to the addition and double-angle formulae. Its note lists possible applications, not additional standalone content requirements that should be duplicated on this statement.
The existing exponential and logarithmic differentiation links cover e^kx, a^kx and ln x; the inner linear factor is represented within the rule rather than as a separate missing concept.
The statement is already linked to the standard-formula mathematical-practice node, which is intentionally excluded from mathematical-knowledge similarity scoring.
The statement already links every explicitly named linear, quadratic, cubic and simultaneous-equation family; no unsupported generic duplicate leaf is needed.
The statement already links the named identities, trigonometric-equation methods, identity proof and identity-based integration concepts.
The existing GEOM-SHAP-TERM-DIAG link exactly represents drawing a diagram from a written geometric description.
The existing prism node explicitly covers solids with uniform cross-section; the note's cylindrical sector is an example of that declared scope, not a separate missing concept.
The existing advanced hyperbolic integration, trigonometric substitution and hyperbolic substitution leaves cover all three standard radical forms and the specified technique.
The existing first-order differential-equation setup, solution, linear ODE modelling and variable-force mechanics links already cover the stated modelling requirement.
The statement already links forming algebraic expressions and simplifying like terms; no broader duplicate leaf is needed.
